experiment: Pythia-ladder causal forge gate (gpt_neox adapter; proxy-train E + full multi-layer forge score, trained vs pinv retained-mAUC)
saes: EleutherAI sparsify sae-pythia-{70m:layers.3,160m:layers.6}-32k (TopK k=16, normalize_decoder=true); host_layer=sae_block+1 (verified by recon cosine)
protocol: identical to causal_host_forge_gate.py (GPT-2): SAE-derived labels, restricted-ReLU task encoder, per-token feed, 200 steps; 70m=3 seeds, 160m=2 seeds.
verdict: The GPT-2 forge-level trained-encoder win does NOT generalize across causal architectures — it reverses to tie/negative on both Pythia rungs. The 'causal hosts win' reading is FALSIFIED. The pattern tracks SAE TYPE / decoder geometry (ReLU+unnormalized decoder -> pinv suboptimal -> trained gains; TopK+normalized decoder -> pinv near-optimal -> trained can't), not host causality. Descriptive; a matched-SAE control (same host, ReLU vs TopK SAE) is the clean isolation. Caveat: the Pythia gate uses a restricted-ReLU task encoder over the TopK SAE's directions (TopK gating dropped for training tractability), so 'SAE type' here means the decoder geometry the dictionary was TRAINED with, not the eval-time activation.
